channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Law of the jungle — 144 replies — 576.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 144 • Updated: 2026-06-15 18:06 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387
>nigger tier envy thread
>/biz/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62366077
>>62359336
why are you seething over someone else's wealth? someone that you don't even know? try it and you either end up dead or in prison either way you still lose.

62366107
>>62366055
materially I am personally comfortable though
But I want it all because I'm not a cuck like you

62366123
>>62365833

No he wouldn't. Power has never been individual and has always been about your ability to build organizations and leverage armies of men, dumbfuck. Can't even edgelord right without sounding like a toddler.

62366125
>Create strife and problems
>Create poor people by infiltrating government and taxing everyone to death and beyond
>"Gommunism will save you!"

62366130
>>62365880

We weren't talking about that, we were basing it off of your retarded little "but I could shoot him" hypothetical. If we're talking straight gang laws Elon Musk is still able to conquer entire micronations, much less have to deal with you lmfao.

That's actually one thing that's always vexed me, how cucked and servile billionaires must be to not leverage their astronomic wealth more in violent ways. Because they can afford to disappear anyone they want.

62366171
>>62366123
>Power has never been individua
BANG BANG BANG
>ACK WHY AM I DYING!
>I AM SUPER NEXT LEVEL RICH WITH CONTROL OF MY DADDIES BANKS AND THE COUNTRY
>I HAVE BILLIONS OF DOLLARS AND HUGE ARMIES OF SECURITY AND CARS
>THEY KILLED ME I WANT JUSTICE!
news says he dies of heart attack and case is closed
>NO NO NO THEY KILLED ME IT WASN'T A HEART ATTACK PLEASE
BANG BANG BANG
>AIIEEEEE MY MONEY TAKE IT!
dies

62366359
>>62366171
we'll smash blokhins record, comrade!

62366421
>>62366107
>I want it all
ok goy. you first. go rob a CEO or whatever dumb fuck move you think is going to make your life great and not just burn everything to the ground.
The reality is you have no power to change your situation.

62366441
>>62366421
Patiently biding my time until the day I may throw my red-hot glowing piece of coal into the palace of Capitalism

62366449
>>62366441
so you're planning on being an economic failure?
If you were able to become rich, you wouldn't want to burn things down.
If you weren't able to become rich, you're too stupid to become rich after a successful revolution.
BTW, pro tip: a successful revolution for retards like you is impossible.

62366459
>>62366449
Don't put words in my mouth you capitalist swine

62366543
>>62366449
how much do make a year?

62368447
>>62366459
you said you were biding your time until you "may" throw a "piece of coal into the palace of Capitalism". Your metaphor suggests a useless action. The biding time suggests that you are going to fail to earn.
>>62366543
Obviously not enough to leave this shithole, and still plenty enough to not infantiley fantasize about le great revolution
>>>>62360788
so you're poor -- otherwise you wouldn't be working. people with money aren't employees. and you defend this?
you're stupid -- otherwise you wouldn't hold idiotic fantasies. people with plans aren't dreamers.
and you still post about this?

62368461
>>62365659

62369006
>>62359336
Ok but why would you do that? Because you are jealous? Or did the rich person rape your mother or something?

62369012
>>62359336
Violence is an incredibly brown phenomen. Just vote socialists into power who will pass tax increases on the rich.

62369030
>>62365772
If we’re being honest here, everyone knows that national socialism type thing is the best system. It doesn’t even need to be tied to just one ethnicity (and nation states actually almost always have different ethnicities living in them, in Germany for example southern germans are mixed with celts and eastern germans with slavs). We could easily have EU level social democracy with tightly regulated, ”only pick the best” kind of immigration.

62369034
>>62369030
*best system for common people i mean. For the elites, USA kind of system with international tax havens is the best.

62369216
>>62359336
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62369231
Let's just recognize this as the pure envy posting that it is. Do nothing drug addicted troon is mad that somebody who is responsible for funding the lives of who knows how many hundreds of thousands of people/families by creating industry and jobs is mad that this person has earned more capital with which they will doubtless continue providing jobs. OP probably personally benefits in ways they aren't even aware of.

62369550
>>62359336
>>62359417
>>62359496
>>62364075
cute

62370324
>>62368461
Reddit is still cringe but unironically got pretty redpilled and revolutionary yeah
They seem more likely do something than 4chan chuds

62370331
>>62359336
cope

62370338
>>62370331
The real cope is asslicking the powerful and kicking down. Wanting to overthrow your slavers is the next step towards liberty at least

62370434
>>62370324
>>62370338
>reddit
>redpilled
>not wanting to destroy literally everything is asslicking
If you are so absolutely hell bent on making this fantasy a reality, why don't you just go start a commune and make it happen? Why does it need to involve murdering people, gaining literally no part of their immense 'stolen' wealth, and giving nothing back to the supposed oppressed workers?
If the utopia can only be built with violence, it will only be upheld with ultraviolence. You won't be the most violent or vicious party around, BTW.

62370741
>>62370434
how do you think our current society is upheld?

62370770
Lmao actual cryptokikes and kikes defending the rich

62370820
>>62361047
>rich
>intelligence
Only room in your bag for one, I'm afraid.

62370830
>>62362126
>let the smart people do the things that make society go
This is what speculators actually believe.

62371364
>>62370741
>upheld
It....isn't?
We had a wildly successful capitalistic system in the 'west' for the last 300 years, and a facsimile of one for the last century (thanks smoke and mirrors fiat), with increasingly onerous compliance demands by the state that start to decouple free markets toward state operated markets (which is far closer to the current paradigm). State regulated markets fucking suck and are a constantly changing landscape of chosen winners and losers based on which group is in power at any one time. Markets are wavering and unstable, currencies are starting to decouple, and global stability is starting to crack under the weight of unnatural (government mandated) migration and capital flows.
Don't ask disingenuous questions, for they open a two way window into your mind (which isn't a compliment)

item.title: 100% VA disability is 4k a month tax free — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
100% VA disability is 4k a month tax free

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-15 18:08 UTC

OP

In the US you would need a NW of about 2 mil to have the same withdrawal power

Early replies (first 3)

62367712
>>62367693
most of those guys did 10-20 years active duty in a bullshit job that nobody wants to do and then spent years and thousands of dollars fighting to get their disability.

I am not jealous of what they had to do to get that money.

62367716
>>62367712
bullshit

some of these guys are 35 y/o and already living the life in Pattaya

62367718
i know it because i met a nigger on Grindr when i was in Pattaya and he told me how he retired at 33 y/o

dude had a massive dick could not even put it inside me so i just sucked it

he was an alcoholic btw, he said he had ptsd but it's hard to believe it

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62367725
>>62367712
WW2 and Korean vets are mostly gone
Vietnam draftees should get gibs because it was horseshit they had to go to the jungle
No additional exceptions and gibs should have been limited in all the towelhead wars nobody was forced to fight

62367738
>>62367712
mcdonalds is a bullshit job no one wants to do and they dont get nearly the same benefits so how does that matter

62367745
>>62367693
best i could do was $20.50/hr
after taxes i barely cleared 2k a month
but yea lets give twice that to people to do nothing, forever, because they were used by the MIC.
god i only wish i could live to see the govt collapse.

62367748
>>62367712
also there hasnt been a real war in ages like the other guy said.. blowing up poor muslim farmers that are in flip flops with ak47s isnt hard or that tough really..

if you fought a real enemy and had to see some shit i guess

62367878
>>62367716
Kek.
>>62367718
Yeah this city is magnet for the worst people in Thailand I like. I won't mention the good places online.

62368252
>>62367718
based fag knower

62368265
>>62367693
Are you the same guy thats posting about va disability every single week and cross posting on /pol/ about it? You're mentally ill

62368274
i get this and im poor

62368282
Low class. Nice. No ownership. Nice.

62369351
>>62367745
>but yea lets give twice that to people to do nothing, forever, because they were used by the MIC.
yes
you can say that the modern military is retarded and pointless at best and the hands of globalist tyrants at worst but any political system which doesn't reward its soldiers handsomely for their service will cease to exist from lack of an army
realistically modern welfare queen soldiers are under compensated if anything where they need to eat shit for years to still get claims denied because having a 105mm going off next to you "isn't service related" while ancient soldiers were the only ones with the right to vote, got free colonial land, or could earn minor titles of nobility

62371164
>>62368274
how are you poor? you literally make the equivalent of a 65k a year wagie. all you have to do is rent some 1br apartment and never eat out.

62371176
>>62367712
Va fraud defense force right on time

Your checks are numbered

62371185
>>62371164
Not only do they get 4k a month they can work full time it doesn't effect their benefit.
They are pondscum I trained a few at a job and one stalked me to another job I had to basically threaten him to fuck off he fell in love with me.
Creeps and pond scum like I said.

62371214
>>62367693
What are the downsides of serving 4 years and getting this for life?

Whenever someone says "we're all equal" tell them about the people getting 4k a month in VA disability doing nothing and about the male pornstar Juan El Caballo Loco who gets paid to fuck milfs all day while wagies toil in the hot sun. Then we'll see who's really equal.

62371225
>>62371185
Yet you still wage all day while he gets his money and you will never do shit about it.

62371282
>>62367693
my obese neighbor had veteran license plates on his spotless ford raptor. he traded it in for a bmw x7 several months ago. it never leaves the driveway so i'm pretty sure he's VA gibsmaxxing.

62371302
>>62371225
he's got 4 fat obese children and a fat pig wife and was paying 2k a month in rent.
I own two houses.

62371329
>>62371185
Shows tits or gtfo. If your a dude your a liar faggot.

62371335
>>62371329
he was some 400lbs ginger manlet I'm a 6'4 chad lanklet, I look a lot like my sister who looks like a female Russel Brand.

62371343
>>62371329
news flash, faggots exist.
You're likely one.

62371360
the people getting full disability are just neeting with a few additional steps. and like neets, they are just a burden, so instead of trying to figure out how to make them useful, we just allow them to sit while the rest of us go on with life.

62371365
Military fairy are like niggers. They are the jesters of society. They deserve monthly gibs

62371371
>>62371360
its sickening seeing how much of my W2 is whisked away to support this bloated state, also largely why I'm pro-FIRE

item.title: Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Questions from a Mid-20’s Investor

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-15 18:10 UTC

OP

Hello /biz/ I will try and keep this brief
Currently I have been investing most of my take home pay simply into VOO as I only just recently started investing
It seemed simple, solid mid-long term option, and sensical for starting out to not fuck up while getting exposure
Now, this has nothing to do with FOMO, but I’m wondering if I should start aiming towards investing in either specific sector ETFs or maybe specific stocks to chase higher returns
My portfolio is currently at $60K mostly in VOO, perhaps with enough DCA I can reach $100k by years end or at least close to that, depends on growth
Currently my total portfolio return ranges from 17-20% depending on the month for context
I’ve seen recently how well things like SOXX have been doing and was considering finding a sector ETF or two to go into since I already have so much broad market exposure
To be clear, this is not me chasing returns based off past performance, I am well aware growth has to come from somewhere and is not infinite so I’m considering CAGRs as far back as possible
With all this in mind, any recommendations? Find a good sector ETF, go into a specific stock, or maybe just focus on getting more income first and then continue to just VOO and chill
And no I don’t wanna sell options doing covered calls or buy your shit coin etc etc
I should add the sectors I’m interested in are semi-conductors, energy (ones that deal with technology needed to cool data centers), and perhaps drones / nuclear for commercial and military usage

Early replies (first 3)

62369867
>>62369835
XLK
SOXL

62369880
>>62369835
Recreate the ETF so you avoid paying management fees.. Not super relevant for VOO, but if you go into niche sector specific ETFs, the management fees are usually over 0.5%

62370000
>>62369867
Not sure how I feel about the triple leverage with SOXL can you red pill me on it, it’s the same deal as QQQ vs TQQQ or whatever the hell it’s called right
But otherwise you’re just advising do some tech and semiconductor either way
>>62369880
Fair point I’ll consider that

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62370048
>>62369835
I need a young emo woman with big titties that loves me unconditionally, fugg. but I'm afraid of intimacy and I have a lot of insecurities about my height, dick size, body odours and I don't know what love is. I have deep appreciation for my family and loved ones but I don't really love them. I'm too schizoid. I miss the touch of a girl

62370061
>>62370048
Did Pomni actually say this?

62370063
>>62370048
Have you tried the Philippines or Belarus?

62370075
>>62370048
>I don't know what love is
its a fantasy concept like magic unironically

62370087
>>62370061
it was in the DVD director commentaries audiotrack
>>62370075
I suspect this as much. I will never live a "normal" life because of it, though

62370103
>>62370048
You need to study hoemath's content thoroughly and if possible invest the 30 bucks into his self max product. I have been watching his live sessions for the past week and boooy oh boy did they open my eyes to the mistakes i was making. Anyways you either work on yourself or drift aimlessly not getting what you want and nees so its up to you big guy

62370125
Yeah looking into it more I think I’ll start DCA a bit into SOXX, if it eventually goes down I know it’ll rebound

62370258
>>62370103
Gee these bots sure are fast to shill paid ads

62370367
Stop talking about Foids please

62370622
>>62369835
>Currently I have been investing most of my take home pay simply into VOO
Just keep doing that anon. You will have better gains than 90% of the anons here.

>>62370048
I had a 20 year old gf a few years ago with g cups and i didnt appreciate tits back then.
I miss this shit now like you wouldnt believe, there are literally 0 women with that big tits without being a lardass out there. I didnt knew what i had bros...

>>62370367
Fuck off. A nice big tiddy gf is very important if you want to be a better trader/investor.

62370823
>>62370622
Fair enough

62370996
>>62370622
Also I only care about Japanese breasts

62371007
>>62370996
You're financially ok with brown nipples?

62371211
>>62371007
A lot are pink
Honestly I’ve never seen a brown nipple before

62371239
>>62371211
pink nip /= nip
anime is not real life chud.
jap girls have brown ones irl

62371250
>>62369835
Honestly man, the amount of effort it takes to beat the SP500 (or NASDAQ, which is even a bit better recently and imo long term from now on) is relatively high. You see countless examples of lifelong financial/investing experts who would have done better if they just did nothing but buy the SP500 and sit on it.

I'm invested more heavily into semiconductors and AI stocks but that's because I have industry knowledge. Industry knowledge can help you determine what is and is not good for long term investing. But then again, I also have industry knowledge in medicine and I won't touch biotech/pharma stocks with a ten foot pole.

62371385
>>62371250
Yeah I was considering just going into QQQ and accepting more ups and downs
I don’t know if it’s the best comparison but comparing it’s CAGR to VOO it has either outperformed or tied with VOO recently
Obviously the down years are way worse but again that’s why I am using CAGR as my comparison basis
It seems like this AI shit isn’t going away any time soon leading to something so focused on the top of the market like QQQ seeming more enticing

item.title: Shiller PE ratio. — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Shiller PE ratio.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-15 18:07 UTC

OP

Is it just a bunch of bullshit?
Are there still big brain anons here?

Early replies (first 3)

62369579
>>62369517
>Is it just a bunch of bullshit?
No, it's just the manipulation can go harder than your expectations.

62369631
>>62369517
The higher the P/E the more future growth is priced in. It's therefor not enough to materialize this growth, because the P/E will then just normalize, and the stock kind of stay flat.. Unless future-future-growth get priced in, that you maintain the P/E while meeting the anticipated growth.

62369645
Tech has been elevated p:e forever, and its a larger and larger part of the index

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62369727
>>62369631
>>62369645
But AI has not yet proven to be profitable. All the ai companies lose money

62369786
>>62369517
>any big brain anons around
I count there are currently at least 3 full blown commie threads up right now in /biz/
What do you think?

62369803
>>62369786
commie threads meaning what? communist bullshit?

62369831
>>62369727
yep thats why we call it the future growth

62371009
>>62369803
>communist bullshit
Is there any other type?
Its not a serious ideology followed by serious people

62371097
>>62369517
They heavily reduced corporate taxes in the last decade

62371118
>>62369517
Multiple countries now have sovereign wealth funds buying in with literal infinite time horizons. Do you think they give a shit about some 40 year PE bullshit when their holding times are literally hundreds of millions of years? Would you rather buy the index now, or in 3026?

62371311
>>62371118
I would rather buy the index when is low. I know that for /biz/ is hard to understand

62371319
>>62371097
And? Finish the thought nigga

62371368
>>62371311
The index is low compared to what it will be in 4052.

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 108 replies — 47.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 108 • Updated: 2026-06-15 15:56 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62364918
>>62361478
He threw a pretty good fastball that one time. It made more progress than the vehicle did thoughbeit.

62365050
>>62361452
because stocks don't behave rationally and memes move the market more strongly than financial schizobabble

62365078
>>62361452
you can become one too, just create a crypto coin, mint 1T tokens and sell 1 for $1 on the open markets

62365099
>>62361452
heres the thing. it was all vaporware the whole time...tesla included. it just wasnt as brazen as the ipo before...worse with every scam. so he could keep borrowing. because hes doing "buy borrow die", it became the banks problem at this point because hes never gonna pay them back, he couldnt if he wanted to, he couldnt if they tried to liquidate. they are heavily incentivized to help him dump the baggies on you to close out these transactions. then he can have a fake ketamine overdose and go party with jeffjeff again.

62365104
>>62361452
elon is one of the only men actually creating value in this world.

almost the entire economy is pure pointless bullshit
>delivering sneakers to bestial niggers
>selling sloppa to bestial niggers
>keeping morbidly obese 100 year old bestial niggers alive
>manufacturing weapons so bestial niggers can shoot each other and blow shit up
>making handbags for the bestial femboons

elon is pursuing the future, the transcendence. he deserves to win.

62365105
>>62365104
They are going to deepfake it all just like nasa with kubrik except this time you paid for it by buying the ipo instead of pillaged wwii wealth

62365111
>>62365105
you are simply a low iq schizoid. you're exactly like the npcs except you're against the current thing.

62365115
>>62365111
You got mkultrad because your uncle molested you while you watched star trek or some other faggot shit and you liked it. Now spaecekeks launches make you feel cummies

62365124
>>62365115
very complex projection. must be something personal.

62365130
>>62365124
Keep fantacizing about scifi bullshit that will never happen. I hope you buy a neuralink and end up like terry schiavo

62365216
>>62365130
low iq npcs such as yourself cannot conceive of something which is not the current thing. you have my pity.

62365235
>>62361452
vibe investing.

62365240
>>62361452
it's because Musk is a cult leader

62365247
>>62361459
Literally what is "dystopian" about swarms of AI powered assassin drones?

62366996
>>62361471
I wish I could make a trillion doing nothing

62367047
Space X is worth 2 trillion...because it just is ok!

62367049
>>62363603
Tesla cars tend to burst in flames more than the most insignificant chink car company.

62367163
>>62361452
Elon Cuck

62367477
>>62361549
lol, >>62361539 can't handle the truth

62367557
every major technological transformation in last 15 years is due to elon.

electric cars = tesla = elon
biohacking = neuralink = elon
space and global broadban = spacex = elon
fixing transporation = boring = elon
AI = openAI (elon founded it) + grok = elon
social media = X = elon

that's why he's a trillionaire and ur a nobody posting a forum when you should be grinding like elon who regularily sleeps on factory floors.

62367687
>>62367557
>gayest cars
>dead monkeys
>global 5G
>jew tunnels
>sloppa
>Jeets
Musk

62367701
>>62367687
Missed the IPO thunderf00t?

62370282
>>62362688
>he received massive subsidies the entire time in tesla
so did everyone else, you braindead commie
t. not a fan

>>62362688
>he received massive
you fucking retarded monkey. commiefornia gave out money left and right for the electric and environmental revolution and he simply lined up. tf is wrong with you. probably a retarded zoomer or something

just because he didn't burn his cash like the entire gaming industry, doesn't make his success not something he didn't earn

you're angry because he would spit in your face and laugh at you if you came up to him with something retarded.
he probably wouldn't, he would save his energy and simply walk away and ignore you like the insignificant vermin that you are

HEYO RETARD ALERT

62370333
>>62362688
here, retarded monkey, because i'm in a good mood
>i forgot to include US-only because i always forget LLMs are only slightly less retarded than you

lol
>i can pull up
yes please do

tesla elon musk subsidies

62370351
>>62362557
>>62363104
>Poorfag doesn't bussines
kek 100%

it's so easy to spot them. the funny thing is they don't even know it is
3 words is all it takes. that's a skill!

62370386
There's quite literally only so many things to invest in and Elon is one of the few guys that make absolutely insane bets on tech that sounds like science fiction. It doesn't matter if he said he'd be sending people to Mars 10 years ago, the fact that his company is constantly pushing forward is impressive. Coming up with insane ideas like putting data centers is space might be completely retarded but he still spends the money for someone to sit down and figure out a less insane version of that and every so often that bet pays off. This is exactly the kind of thing that the US did during WW2 and the cold war and its how they ended up landing on the moon, build the Internet, the blackbird, develop nuclear bombs as well as nuclear energy, and so on. The only problem with Elon is that he's a cringe spergy attention whore.

62370487
>>62370386
Oh and his obsession with H1B indians is bad as well.

62370524
>>62361478
He doesn't even play poe2 lmao. He literally streamed poe2 to thousands of people on a character he paid a chinese guy to level up, so that he could appear on top of the hardcore ladder.
What kind of a man does that? Who the fuck would think that is sane? He literally does blatant shit like this, lies to your face.

62370572
>>62361478
xai literally hasn't done anything

62370604
>>62370487
he already got bodied for his opinion in late 2024. everyone did

item.title: having a girlfriend was the costliest mistake I have ever done. — 26 replies — 47.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
having a girlfriend was the costliest mistake I have ever done.

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-06-15 17:38 UTC

OP

having a girlfriend was the costliest mistake I have ever done. all my free time GONE, I could no longer work on myself or my goals without her worrying im "cheating" on her. my savings dwindled. my sanity run through the dirt.

you guys were right

Early replies (first 3)

62368718
Mid guys get mid girls. Sorry anon.

62368731
>>62368715
Sounds like a skill issue. My gf cooks, cleans, and rarely asks me for money. She's also supportive of my chess and gym hobbies.

Here's the high protein dinner she made for me last night.

62368741
>>62368731
its undercooked

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62368834
>>62368715

62368839

62368841
>>62368839

62368849
>>62368715
>>62368718
>>62368731
>>62368834

62368851
>>62368834

people like him should NOT reproduce, so the system is working. We don't need selfish children who can't think of other people.

62368859
>>62368715

62368880

62368895

62368932

62368984

62368996
>>62368984

62369037

Woman sabotage men because improving themselves takes actual effort.

item.title: So when is SPCX going to crash? — 25 replies — 39.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
So when is SPCX going to crash?

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-15 17:33 UTC

OP

> Next FOMC meeting
> Next earnings date
> Right when it gets into the Nasdaq100
> ?
There is no way that a company priced in hopium that actually loses money is not going to crash like a shitcoin at some point.

Early replies (first 3)

62366003
>>62365854
When is TSLA going to crash?

Musk can string out the SPCX hopium even longer than the TSLA hopium, possibly for decades.

62366023
>>62365854
I expect that every test launch of a SuperHeavy/Starship will create an options trader's dream.
* rocket blows up, stock price plunges
* test goes perfectly, stock price zooms

So, just trade straddles starting a day or two before every big event.

Ever seen Boeing's stock price after a crash? One plane goes down, whole stock drops. Never mind that it's usually either defective maintenance or the pilots' fault.

62366153
>>62365854
Look at this as you'd look at a shitcoin TGE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62366334
>>62366153
Shitcoins crash so fucking hard when they do.

62366341
>>62366003
Tesla actually makes money, Spacex is a bunch of AI space datacenters dream bullshit. Only Starlink makes any money in it.

62366378
It will never be below 135. Only low iq brainlests that don’t understand how important mass/cost to orbit is and how important orbital refueling is think it like mega epic overvalued

Oh and SpaceX currently has no heavy lift competition. Only Amazon is in the field and their rocket exploded recently, they are years behind. Other competitors like rklb and fly can barely do small lift. Rklb is still working on neutron to compete with falcon on medium lift. SpaceX is a monopoly currently that will unlock the next frontier. But yeah you’re right, there is nothing to do in space and its just going to crash

62366436
>>62366378
Nigga once the money tap breaks down the market gets fucking fair very fucking quick.
Tesla has done -75% in the past.

62366443
>>62366003
Tesla makes cars, ai, robots, charging stations, solar panels, power batteries and electrical grid technology.
It wont.

62366451
>>62366436
Okay? It has also do 25000% growth since 2013. Your rebuttal?

62366518
>>62366451
The point is to figure out when it is going to crash to buy very low. If you want to get it now that is trading at infinite pe ratio go ahead.

62366779
>>62365854
Even if its not vaporware, these projects all take decades. There will be multiple overall market crashes/correctioba before they get anything done. Being a single stock, its gonna crash more often then the whole market. 100% chance of a dump right before any big got contracts are announces too so insiders can load up.

62366790
When all of Reddit and biz thinks something’s gonna crash, it’s probably gonna moon

Not in the stock but am now tempted for a short term position

62367082
>>62365854
The index inclusion narrative is getting frontrun. SPCX will go 30% up from its IPO price and go down from there

62367245
>>62365854
Just wait for another rocket explosion.
Buy the dip.
This shit is so easy.

62369275
>>62366378
Sending shit to space doesn't rely on the value of stocks. It's going to trade up and down. It will probably dump soon when people are allowed to sell and/or when the earnings come in.

Buy when it comes down. If they can actually successfully make data centers in space the value will moon.

62369319
>>62366790
This. I bought hundreds of shares based solely on Reddit tears.

62369336
>>62365854
i give it a couple of weeks

62369347
Retail are idiots that buy no matter what because they have no hope of a future otherwise. Fundamentals literally don't matter anymore, really they haven't since 2023

62369355
the only way it crashes is if the 10 million starlink users stop using it and that doesn't sound plausible, i mean when califortards started burning teslas, the stock went down but came back up because it was a nothing burger local skirmish, it turns out politics and business don't mix, don't let the leftard musk hate shilling inform your financial decisions, most of them are delusional hypocrites, very few of them--in fact this proves there's very few of them--have any practical convictions

62369365
>>62365854
Space X is hopeless endeavor since moment when Elon decided land on tower!

13 or so flights and not even Orbit?
Once lucky landing?
Since nothing ..except blowing up money!

Meantime Blue Origin despite latest seatback Land on second try and deploy satellite?

X is just coupium !

Elon not going even to Moon anywhere beyond 2050!

It is all hype and money scam !
You should understand that by now!

62370328
>>62369365
Retarded plebbit spacing opinion.

>SpaceX has achieved over 670 successful launches across its rocket families, maintaining one of the highest launch cadences and success rates in aerospace history.

>Falcon 9: The world's most active workhorse has launched 662 times with a 99.55% success rate. Its active version, the Falcon 9 Block 5, boasts an even higher 99.83% success rate, with only one single in-flight failure across hundreds of flights

>Falcon Heavy: Composed of three Falcon 9-derived cores, this heavy-lift vehicle has completed all of its operational flights with a 100% success rate since its highly publicized 2018 debut.

>Starship: The massive, fully reusable super-heavy launcher is actively undergoing rapid, iterative test flights. The May 2026 inaugural test flight of the upgraded Starship Version 3 (V3) successfully launched, deployed 20 dummy satellites, and completed a controlled atmospheric re-entry and splashdown in the Indian Ocean

62370340
>>62370328
? Profit

62370936
>>62365854
Why to people buy businesses that make no money? Where do they think does the money come from?

62371151
>>62366378
You sound retarded. The way spaceX is priced it’s an AI startup with a rocket hobby. The valuation is fucking crazy and rigged to manipulate passive index funds. It’s absolutely priced in at best case scenario future happening. OpenAI is going to do the same thing. I’m absolutely furious this shit was given the green light

62371179
>>62370328
Daily reminder only 4.3% of space X shares are publicly traded. Enjoy your future rugpull

item.title: How do I fully Divest from the USA — 45 replies — 37.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do I fully Divest from the USA

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-06-15 16:58 UTC

OP

Anyone who’s still investing in U.S markets at this point is abetting fascism. I want a total European portfolio to support Liberal democracy

Early replies (first 3)

62368148
I unironically do this and it's easy.

Just a case of EU+UK tracker ETFs + Canada and Australia + Japan-Taiwan-SK. I balance it out with pan-European IT and defence ETFs to balance out the overabundance of financials and industrials.

This gives you a portfolio of liberal democracies.

62368154
>>62368132
>fascism

62368162
>>62368154
You’re just uneducated on history

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62369072
As for the rest of Asia I am skipping Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand and Vietnam. While the first two might be cheap, I think the muslim will not deliver. Thailand is a failling society infested by the decadent spirit of globojudaism (same for the Phillipines) and Vietnam is inaccessable by retail investors. I would be careful with picking passive tracker for a market like Vietnam. I also think all of these SEA monkeys can be well covered by SG banks like DBS and UOB. Now... Chinese equities.. Where do I start. It's best to get a brocker with access to IBKR. Then you need to screen for stocks with good dividends, stable or growing cash flow. The chinese markets do have the same retarded lot system like the japs, but the prices are lower, which means you don't need 15,000 euro to buy the minimum lot. The interesting thing about Chyna is that many companies are listed both onshore and in HK. In HK market the prices are lower, often 20-30% lower, mostly because the westards are running huge short positions on them. A good portfolio with Chyna stocks will consists of some state banks, because that's how the economy functions, the big banks are a great bet, they won't fall like the western. My tip is China Merchants Bank, Bank of China (or also it's subsidiary Bank of China Hong Kong), Ping An Insurance (which owns a bank Ping An Bank). If you buy them in HK, you get more dividend for lower price. As for mainlan stocks, here are my tips: Midea Group or Haier Smarthome (absolute dominators with lots of strategic advantages vs the westards), CATL (dominant and very efficient), Hikvision (camers and automation/robotics), Hengdian DMEGC Magnetics (magnets and solar energy), China Telecom (Telco and cloud computing), Nari Technology, Goneo Group (basically Legrand of China). Don't forget to buy some gold too!

62369185
>>62369067
Ok Chang, go eat a dog burger or whatever lol

62369190
>>62368132
What's PDA?

62369208
>>62369190
Having sex at the workplace

62369243
>>62368216
>dictatorial
>theocracy
>rounded up in camps and killed
GENUINELY what the fuck are you referring to?

62369274
>>62369243
NTA, but sounds like he is talking about Project 2025.

62370119
You shouldnt divest away from strategic US sectors. If the US falls, the last thing on your mind will be your portfolio, so an investment in the US is an investment into the future.

62370160
>>62368132
>fighting for their rights to perform PDA at work
Lmao for fucks sake man... Euros are such a bunch of degenerate faggots

62370630
>>62368132
Whatever companies you buy, you have to make sure they dont use any products from sp500 comapnies. Good luck!

62370666
>>62368132
Only if you hate money. Even European defense companies are down this year. America and the AI companies are just dominating everything.

America is evil but Satan and his jews rule this world and have ALL the money.

62370699
I hope no one ITT lets his investments be influenced by posts containing the words "Jewsa", "pozzed", "Jewmericans" or "Satan"

62370702
>>62368132
>this is the difference between us
having creeps in your workplace?

62370714
Its impossible, even if you only invest in thirdie companies their biggest customer is burger. Even if you only invest in sanctioned countries they use third parties to sell to burgers

62370718
>>62370714

62370721
>>62370160
It's just gonna be bunch of gays "expressing" themselves.

62370725
>>62368132

62370726
>>62370630
>>62370160
>>62370119

>>62368132
Isn't Spain giving workers breaks to masturbate?
Kekaroonies.

62370733
I wonder if there are people as retarded as OP but unironically.
I'm glad I sold all my EU crap at a + because this shit is going up in flames.

62370754
This place is fucking dying and we are being ruled over by burectautic women and boomers stuck in yesterday. Do not put money in here. This place will be a testaments what happens when retarded libs take over and encourage societies to rot in the name of progressivism

62370778
>>62368148
Thanks bro, i’ve been wondering how to invest so that my investments both generate money and strenghten european enlightenment.

62370787
>>62368279
UK is more like USA than like rest of europe.

62370799
>>62368999
Enjoy sucking Putin’s dick like your country already did for many years, retard. It’s either the union or sucking either Trump’s or Putin’s dick.

62370817
>>62368132
But what if i want to abet fascism?

62370824
>>62370754
I wonder when the first big cunt takes a look at Germany's raw numbers from 2022 onward and nopes the fuck out. And the EU is just Germany / German money on a proxy.

62370828
>>62370725
>>62370666
>>62370119
Kek americans coping hard here. You guys got lucky with the world wars but just because you got lucky one doesn’t mean you will keep outperforming everything forever. Everyone tells you to keep buying at the top.

62370935
>>62368132
Listen here you capitalistic chud. In Europe we DO NOT invest, that’s for capitalist American pigs. What you MUST do with your left over money is hand it over to the brave and hardworking souls from North Africa who need our help now more than ever. We OWE them and it’s YOUR personal responsibility to be held accountable for the actions of your ancestors, and provide them with proper care and a bright livelihood. So go donate as much all your left over money that you don’t even need, only a facist scumbag would do otherwise.

62370975
>>62370935
>account based in indorussia

62370977
>>62368132
lol

62370980
>>62370754
The wealthy men of today are a bunch of dysgenic narcissists as well, so with the world having no means of justice in this generation, evacuating the entire North American continent and sending everybody back to where they came from is starting to look like a relatively appealing outcome at this point. It would at least establish how completely unacceptable this society was and that its history should never repeat again.

62370981
>>62368132
Have fun getting raped by taxes to fund gender affirming care and immigrants.

item.title: I work for an RIA managing assets and helping clients w/ financial planning AMAA >bought gold at $1900 >IBIT in my retir… — 41 replies — 24.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I work for an RIA managing assets and helping clients w/ financial planning AMAA >bought gold at $1900 >IBIT in my retir…

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-06-15 16:29 UTC

OP

I work for an RIA managing assets and helping clients w/ financial planning AMAA
>bought gold at $1900
>IBIT in my retirement accounts
>catastrophe bonds are my favorite asset class

Early replies (first 3)

62366984
>>62366943
i'm going DCA on 80% VTI 20% XSD until cows come home, and then exiting XSD entirely if the semiconductor sector goes completely tits-up
how does this risk profile compare to the recommended amount, assuming that i'm already tolerant of short-term drawdowns in favor of long term growth?

62367052
>>62366984
Depends on your time horizon, I'll assume you're young (<30) so a typical advisor would tell you that's an appropriate risk tolerance (100% us equity with an industry tilt). Most target date funds for a 2060 retirement are 90%+ equity right now.

The "rule of thumb" is 100 or 120 minus your age = how much % stocks you are overall.

Also, if you look into something called the "efficient frontier" the basic idea is the units of risk you take on per unit of return past 85% stocks is dog.

I'm worried about regime changes though, in which case stocks could have a decade+ of awful returns

62367065
>>62367052
>Also, if you look into something called the "efficient frontier" the basic idea is the units of risk you take on per unit of return past 85% stocks is dog.
5-15% bond ETF into the mix then? there's not much i can diversify into since my net worth is trash.

>I'm worried about regime changes though, in which case stocks could have a decade+ of awful returns
what would be the damage mitigation strategy for pure ETF portfolios? sell some (or all) and dip into bond ETFs?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62367187
>>62367173
I owe it to anon, this is my penance

62367230
>>62367213
HYSA isn't bad if you don't know horizon. 3% risk free is great.

> I have just been saving for shit like that, unexpected expenses
This is literally what insurance is for bro, 2% deductible and they buy you a new roof. I just went through that for hail damage.

How much does it cost a month all-in to exist?

62367266
>>62367052
>units of risk you take on per unit of return
One thing I've never understood from asset managers, everyone always says learn risk analysis, manage risk vs. reward, etc. I get it. But
HOW DO YOU MEASURE UNITS OF RISK AND RETURN????
Just give me some typical examples so I know what to look into. No one ever explains this.

62367326
>>62367184
>Maybe consider something that has these classes represented by ETF, example %s that I'd do:
thanks for the insight.
>ILS
what is this and why does the graph sawtooth like that?

62367364
>>62367266
It's actually super easy, return is real return before taxes and "risk" is volatility aka standard deviation.

>>62367247
Bummer for that experience, but it could have been bad luck and it was a long time ago - regulations have grown stricter for insurance since then. Consider that your lived experience could be making you biased.

That being said, having a phat AF emergency fund can help you sleep at night, but 6 months is good and 12 months is phat and over 12 months is inefficient. Leads to why I asked about spending rate.

$1950/month x 130% because margin of safety and most people spend a little more on maintenance and shit than they think (property taxes?) = $~2,500/m so $30k a year living expenses.

You have over 4 years in what most would consider an efund man. It's pretty inefficient.

Think about meeting halfway and keeping $60k in the HYSA imo.

The rest can go into a taxable or even a Roth IRA if you AGI isn't over the MAGI threshold. Roths are VERY flexible on taking out contributions (any time, any reason) and give great tax advantages to the growth if you make it to 60.

The other option is a taxable brokerage (max flexibility, but no tax advantage). You could get over 3% pretty easily with minimal risk taken on.

50% VT, 20% BSV, 20% JPST, 10% ILS would give you such a nicer return with a hilariously low max drawdown. You could backtest it on portfolio visualizer to see, but would have to replace the ILS with BSV to get a decent backtest.

62367445
>>62367326
ILS is a catastrophe bond ETF. They buy all kinds of catastrophe bonds from around the world for different natural disasters. Catastrophe bonds are floating rate (no interest rate risk) bonds that only pay principal back if the named disaster doesn't occur.

Example: You can buy a Louisiana catastrophe bond from State Farm insurance and you don't get your principal back if a hurricane hits the state and it might pay something like 10% a year in interest. The insurance company is letting you "buy the risk".

ILS buys all kinds of cat bonds so is diversified enough that you don't get fucked. It's an investment category that as a result has NO CORRELATION to any other asset class. It doesn't move with anything which is amazing for portfolio building.

>why does the graph sawtooth like that?
That's the NAV going down proportionally to the fat af 1% a month dividend payments.

62367457
>>62367445
amazing. thanks for your insight and explanation.

62367509
>>62367457
Catastrophe bonds are a seriously unknown asset class for most people and absolutely incredible. Near equity returns with no correlation is insanity for fixed income. In 10 years or so they will be mainstream but until they the premium is nice to collect.

CBYYX for mutual funds and ILS for ETFs are what I target.

https://47598668.hs-sites.com/hubfs/ILS%20ETF%20Fund%20Documents/ILS%20ETF%20Fact%20Sheet.pdf?hsCtaAttrib=189598987686

62367586
>>62366943
I also work as an advisor for an RIA. Does your firm act as a 3(38) fiduciary?

>I bought silver at $17.50 and sold at $103

62367634
>>62367586
>Does your firm act as a 3(38) fiduciary?
We don't, but we do give general recommendations for 401k accounts outside of our management.

62367655
>>62367364
>It's actually super easy, return is real return before taxes and "risk" is volatility aka standard deviation.
So you're saying for a stock to hit X price, the return = X - current price and risk is measured as number of standard deviations from current price to X?

62367658
>>62367445
>buy the risk
So it's like a bet. Isn't it exactly what crashed housing market in 2008?

62367683
>>62367655
Risk is defined as the uncertainty of future returns. It can be measure by volatility, or how wide the returns are historically. The statistical measure of this is standard deviation.

Ignore real returns for this, it account for inflation. Return is just (ending total return - beginning total return)/beginning total return. The S&P 500 for example gives 8%-10% a year usually, depends on the time period you look at. It includes dividends/interest AND price appreciation.

The S&P 500 also has a great deal of risk. The worst returns are -40% per year and the best returns are +50% per year. That's a wide range of returns so a very wide standard deviation relative to most other asset classes.

Investors expect a high long-term return as compensation for the variability in yearly returns which is why stonks do so well as an asset class if you zoom out far enough. Real question is should you/could you stomach the volatility?

62367721
>>62367658
All investments take on risk except for HYSA, 3 month US treasuries, and money market funds in theory. Those are the "risk free" rate of return.

With catastrophe bonds you're betting on a lot of different natural disasters not happening in a lot of different places at once.

If you buy regular investment grade bonds you're betting on the US government or big corporations paying you back and not getting fucked by inflation while you wait (reinvestment risk, interest rate risk, default risk).

If you buy stocks you're betting on those companies being profitable in the future (a bajillion different risks).

If you buy real estate you're betting on inflation and low interest rates.

Every investment has inherent risk. The more risk you take on the higher your expected long-term return is since risk = return.

2008 crashed because the Clinton administration coerced banks into giving loans to people that couldn't pay them back. It took a few years to come due but then everyone defaulted on the mortgages they should have never been approved for in the first place and the whole system almost collapsed.

62367729
>>62367683
I appreciate that you're trying to help but why are you people always so vague? Can't you just tell me the formula as I asked here >>62367655 or can you just give me one example of the exact return and risk values of something? I see you started to do that with S&P but
>8-10%
>great deal of risk
>wide range of returns
you sound like you're just eyeballing this in every case. Do you have some exact set of steps you follow to calculate these? Are your "units of risk and return" actually defined???

>Risk is defined as the uncertainty of future returns. It can be measure by volatility, or how wide the returns are historically. The statistical measure of this is standard deviation.
>Investors expect a high long-term return as compensation for the variability in yearly returns which is why stonks do so well as an asset class if you zoom out far enough. Real question is should you/could you stomach the volatility?
I know... This is 101 and doesn't address my questions at all.

62367855
>>62367729
Not trying to frustrate you man, just wasn't sure what you were exactly asking for. Attached is actual standard deviation calcs I ran on last 26 years of data for different asset classes. These will change depending on time period you look at.

SHARPE ratio is what you want. This measures return per unit of risk. Here's the formula: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sharpe_ratio

You can probably see why I didn't just post the gibberish formula.

You can calculate standard deviation on your own in excel with the STDEV formula on a data set. The reason it changes is because the standard deviation will change depending on the time period you look at.

>you sound like you're just eyeballing this in every case. Do you have some exact set of steps you follow to calculate these? Are your "units of risk and return" actually defined???

I am note. I pull historical returns and calculate historical standard deviation and return then use those to calculate sharpe ratio. You can do it in excel if you get the data.

62367865
>>62367729
im an economist and everything he says there is common knowledge. dyor a bit. he makes good threads by biz standards

62368104
>>62367855
Thanks again. I've seen Sharpe ratio before just never had anyone explain when/how it's actually applied. I'm an amateur mathematician and CSfag so exact algorithm and I/O is what I was looking for and you got it. How do you decide the timeframe when calculating stddev? Do you just go with other historical periods with similar conditions to present? Do you compare multiple cases like typical years vs. a repeat of 2008 or something? Anything else?

>>62367865
Guess no one in this thread can read. I literally said in that post what he said.is all 101.

62368125
>>62368104
>I've seen Sharpe ratio before just never had anyone explain when/how it's actually applied.
etc is all part of the 101 which is why i said what I said. Its just encouragement to (e.g.) read the book he shared. anon may not be around for your next set of Q's.

62368133
>>62368104
choosing timeframe is an art/science thing but i wont speak for OP

62368140
>>62368125
Work on your ego, brother.

62368152
>>62368140
i think you should word on your reading skills if you tyhought that was arrogant. i have RSI iwas typing an answer for you but forget it then

62368159
>>62368104
>How do you decide the timeframe when calculating stddev?
I go as far back as I can as long as the data is reliable with 1972 as a hard stop start year.

1972 because the Nixon shock was '71, which started our current financial system.

As far back as I can go because it captures all the different regimes. Inflation/interest rates mostly. We have been in a low interest rate, low inflation regime for awhile which is NOT normal, it'll change (or already has) soon.

Shorter, near term history is useful too. SOx Act, Dodd-Frank, etc are all relatively recent and changed the regulatory environment. It's a balance of what happened in the past in other regimes way back, what happened recently under current regime/regulation, and then trying to predict what happens next. So
>1972-present
>trailing 30 years
>trailing 10 years

Those are the most useful for me.

>Do you just go with other historical periods with similar conditions to present
Related, I actually want time periods w/ different conditions. I want to know what asset classes did great during the fucked up periods in the 80s when mortgages were 18% and when we had stagflation last (very relevant soon probably). Stocks did balls during that.

>Do you compare multiple cases like typical years vs. a repeat of 2008 or something? Anything else?
Yes, a "typical" year will be 1 standard deviation since inception to measure risk, that's where it gets super useful. You can show a client how much the S&P 500 has gone up & down 1 STD (2/3 of all years) in a given time period and see how much risk they can stomach.

We also show them the worst case years for different portfolios, which are all 2008 or 2022 this regime. Helps them figure out how much drawdown they can take on before vomiting.

62368169
lil nigger asking basics too good to read the fuknig manual

62368175
>>62368133
That's a really good way to put it imo, trying to use history to predict the future is the best we have but there's nuance to selecting timeframe. That's why I pick multiple.

62368217
>>62368159
Awesome, thanks m80

62370694
>>62366943
Anon, are you still here? Can you explain why some ETFs offer insane dividends?

https://stockanalysis.com/etf/mstw/dividend/

62370755
>>62366943
>>catastrophe bonds are my favorite asset class
Let me introduce you to my buddy lucly larry...he needs some building insurance.

62370788
>>62366943
>MDD 82%.
Is that even allowed? i thought that any drawdown higher than 30% was taboo. If i was your customer and seen one asset going down 82% i would skin you alive.

62370826
>>62370694
Because they pay you back your own money and the nav decreases. If you give me 100 bucks and its based on as asset that grows 10% a year, I can pay you 20% but now you only have 90% of what you started with. Completely disregard anyone selling you this shit. There is nothing that pays weekly that is a good investment, and most shit paying monthly i trash. Anything yielding more than 5% is unlikely to keep up with inflation. You could have asked google btw...ngmi
